conditions.name: essential hypertension
conditions.note: Well controlled on lisinopril 10 mg daily. BP 132/84 at goal. No medication changes.

conditions.name: hyperlipidemia
conditions.note: LDL 128 mg/dL borderline elevated, triglycerides 160 mg/dL mildly elevated. Patient continues to prefer dietary management over statin. New lab values reviewed, continued conservative management plan confirmed.

conditions.name: acute sinusitis
conditions.note: Previously active condition from January 2023, treated with amoxicillin. Confirmed fully resolved with no residual symptoms. Clinical status changed from active to resolved.

medications.name: lisinopril 10 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take one 10 mg tablet by mouth once daily for hypertension

medications.name: fish oil 1000 mg oral capsule
medications.sig: take one 1000 mg capsule by mouth once daily

immunizations.name: recombinant zoster vaccine
immunizations.note: Dose 1 of 2-dose series. VIS provided and reviewed. No immediate adverse reaction during 15-minute observation. Second dose scheduled in 2-6 months.

document_references.description: Annual wellness visit note for 54-year-old male including complete 14-point review of systems, comprehensive physical examination, review of fasting lab results (lipid panel, A1c, glucose, CMP), PHQ-2 depression screening (negative), immunization administration (Shingrix dose 1), assessment of hypertension (well controlled), hyperlipidemia (continued dietary management), resolved sinusitis, and preventive care plan with follow-up in 12 months.

diagnostic_reports.description: CMP drawn fasting at visit. Only creatinine (0.92 mg/dL), potassium (4.1 mEq/L), and eGFR (>90) are explicitly reported in the narrative. Remaining CMP components not individually enumerated. Renal function normal per provider assessment.
